rag_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be placed in component implementation scopes for implementation-level bindings.
Assumptions and guarantees for component ports belong in component type scopes, not implementations.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by using only visible identifiers from the current component scope.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or data types not present in the analyzed model.
Generated AGREE code must align with the authoritative AADL model structure.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Implementation-level bindings require valid subcomponent and connection references.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type scopes.
Do not reference AADL properties as AGREE variables without explicit typed ports or constants.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE assume/guarantee syntax and validator-driven repair patterns
Model analyst component type and implementation extraction
